title: Google Launches Gemini 3.5 Flash: Frontier Intelligence at 4x the Speed
subtitle: New model beats GPT-5.5 and Claude Opus 4.7 on benchmarks while running four times faster — and at less than half the cost
excerpt: At Google I/O 2026, Google unveiled Gemini 3.5 Flash, its most capable and fastest model yet. Available today across all Google products and APIs, it sets a new bar for intelligence-per-dollar.
seo_title: Google Gemini 3.5 Flash: Faster Than GPT-5.5, Cheaper Than Everything
seo_description: Google's Gemini 3.5 Flash launches at Google I/O 2026 — 4x faster output than competing frontier models, higher benchmark scores, and less than half the price. Available today.
body:
The New Speed King at the Frontier

Google used its I/O 2026 keynote stage to ship the model it has been running internally for months: Gemini 3.5 Flash. Starting today it is available to all users across Google products and to developers via the Gemini API — and based on the numbers Google put on screen, it has a legitimate claim to being the best price-performance frontier model in the industry.

The headline stat is output speed: Gemini 3.5 Flash produces tokens four times faster than comparable frontier models such as GPT-5.5 and Claude Opus 4.7, while still sitting at the top right of the intelligence-versus-speed chart Google displayed on stage. It also made dramatic gains on GDPVal, Artificial Analysis's benchmark for economically valuable real-world tasks.

What Changed from 3.1 Pro

  • Benchmark improvements across nearly every category when compared to Gemini 3.1 Pro — Google's previous top model.
  • Coding saw the largest jumps, particularly on agentic coding tasks and long-horizon workflows.
  • Context and reasoning improvements that make it Google's first model optimized end-to-end for agentic operation rather than single-shot Q&A.

The Cost Argument

Google made an explicit pitch to enterprise CFOs: at less than half the price of comparable frontier models, switching 80% of a workload from alternatives to 3.5 Flash could save a company processing one trillion tokens a day more than $1 billion annually. That is not a theoretical number — it is the math for the scale Google says top enterprise customers are already operating at.

The company noted that many companies are on pace to blow through their annual token budgets by midyear. Flash is positioned as the relief valve.

Internal Validation

Google says it has been dogfooding 3.5 Flash inside its own Antigravity AI developer platform. In March, internal usage was at half a trillion tokens a day. By I/O it had grown to more than three trillion tokens per day — doubling every few weeks. That scale created a feedback loop that shaped the final model.

Gemini 3.5 Pro: Coming Next Month

Flash is the first model in the 3.5 family. Gemini 3.5 Pro — the deeper reasoning variant — is being used internally now and is scheduled to ship to developers next month. Google said it shows "great improvements" without specifying benchmarks.

Availability

Gemini 3.5 Flash is live today via the Gemini API, in the Gemini app, and powering the new Antigravity 2.0 developer platform. Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers get access immediately. Enterprise pricing and quota details are available in the Google AI developer docs.
